mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive forward returns do not significantly exceed baseline during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with a Rank IC > 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary easing, a rebound in the index of industrial production signals recovering factory output and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth during easing cycles yields negative or flat 5d forward returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 1.0%, further rate cuts provide diminishing marginal stimulus to equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as markets price in economic weakness.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising unemployment and declining total nonfarm payrolls confirms a broad labor market deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS shows a Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, accelerating real GDP growth reflects strong economic momentum and corporate profit expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth during recovery phases fails to generate positive 20d returns in > 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing activity and higher corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO growth remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60d forward returns are negative in > 55% of growth periods.
